summary:
Thin bank-research window again: just 5 of 60 tracked tweets carried a bank/strategist tag; the remaining 55 were untagged general-market chart posts (overwhelmingly Mike Zaccardi's live-tape commentary, plus HFI Research's Strait of Hormuz tanker-tracking thread and The Chart Report). No dailychartbook, LanceRoberts, @neilksethi, or @neilksethinew posts appeared in this collection cycle.

BofA leads with 2 items: HFI Research's Hormuz tanker-traffic update (tagged BofA) on the US-Iran escalation and the emerging oil-product deficit, and @eliant_capital's crowded-positioning chart ('everyone's back on one side of the boat').

Goldman Sachs contributes 2 items via Mike Zaccardi: the US economic surprise index back to flatline, and hyperscaler AI capex needing downstream profit-pool capture to justify returns.

Apollo (Torsten Slok) contributes 1 item on oil and yields decoupling, flagging the Fed's inflation problem shifting from headline to core.

**By bank:** BofA 2, Goldman 2, Apollo/Slok 1.

banks.theme: BofA -- Strait of Hormuz tanker-flow disruption feeding the oil deficit, and crowded consensus positioning

items.author: HFI_Research
items.text:
Strait of Hormuz over the last 24 hours.

With the US and Iran now trading blows, there were no US escorts in the last 24 hours. Inbound VLCCs unrelated to Iran fell to zero for the last 2 days.

The key to the oil math was always inbound tankers, not outbound tankers. With no incoming VLCCs, production shut-in will rise, which is the essence of the oil market deficit.

As you will see in the @MarineTraffic video, AIS spoofing appears about halfway through. According to @MenchOsint 's reporting today, IRGC speed boats turned vessels around in the Oman lane. This will also prevent shadow transits from taking place.

With US conducting the largest strike since the ceasefire took place in April, it will be interesting to see what develops further.

I have included my inbound tanker list below for the last 3 days.

items.author: eliant_capital
items.text: Everyone’s back on one side of the boat… https://t.co/PQVCo1FUl5

banks.theme: Apollo (Torsten Slok) -- oil-yield decoupling signals the Fed's inflation problem has shifted from headline to core

items.author: MikeZaccardi
items.text:
When oil and yields stop moving together, it signals the Fed's problem has shifted from headline to core... cheaper oil alone won't open the door to cuts

-Torsten at Apollo https://t.co/FlNpNS4Ygh
